festivals.name: Gion Matsuri
festivals.description: Japan's grandest festival fills Kyoto for the entire month of July, culminating in spectacular float processions.

festivals.name: Awa Odori
festivals.description: Japan's largest dance festival draws over a million spectators to watch 100,000 dancers in the streets.

destinations.name: Tokyo
destinations.description: Japan's electric capital blends futuristic districts like Akihabara with ancient temples and world-class cuisine.

destinations.name: Kyoto
destinations.description: Japan's cultural heartland with 17 UNESCO sites, tea ceremony culture, and over 1,600 Buddhist temples.

destinations.name: Osaka
destinations.description: Japan's kitchen is famous for takoyaki, okonomiyaki, and a vibrant street food culture around Dotonbori.

destinations.name: Hiroshima & Miyajima
destinations.description: The Peace Memorial Museum and the iconic floating torii gate of Itsukushima Shrine on Miyajima Island.

destinations.name: Nara
destinations.description: Japan's first capital where hundreds of freely roaming deer share space with UNESCO World Heritage temples.

destinations.name: Hakone
destinations.description: Classic Japanese hot spring resort with Mt Fuji views, an open-air sculpture museum, and traditional ryokan.

destinations.name: Nikko
destinations.description: Elaborate shrine complex in alpine forests featuring the lavishly ornate Tosho-gu mausoleum of Tokugawa Ieyasu.

destinations.name: Hokkaido
destinations.description: Japan's northern wilderness with dramatic national parks, fresh seafood, and powder-snow ski resorts.
